scan.version_anchor: q-e/include/qe_version.h: version_number = '7.5' (helpdoc-generated INPUT_*.txt files in PHonon/Doc were built at 7.4; source is authoritative)

entries.name: cell-parameters
entries.quantity: Bravais lattice: ibrav + celldm, or explicit lattice vectors
entries.units: celldm(1)=alat in bohr; CELL_PARAMETERS card in alat|bohr|angstrom per its option; internally at(:,:) stored in alat units

artifact.name: pw.x input (&SYSTEM ibrav/celldm, CELL_PARAMETERS card)
artifact.format_notes: celldm(1) is the lattice parameter a in BOHR. With ibrav=0, vectors come from CELL_PARAMETERS with unit option alat|bohr|angstrom ('bohr' assumed if omitted, deprecated). The same cell is propagated verbatim into every dyn file and flfrc header (celldm + optional 'Basis vectors' block in alat units).

source_anchors.what: celldm doc: alat = celldm(1) is the lattice parameter a (in BOHR)
CELL_PARAMETERS card, unit enum alat|bohr|angstrom
dyn-file header re-emits ntyp, nat, ibrav, celldm(1:6)

operation.notes: Defines the periodic cell in which FC2 and D(q) are expressed. All downstream q-vectors are quoted in 2pi/alat units of this cell.

maps_to.notes: QE consumes full lattice vectors; the existing catalog node is only the contracted CellVolume (omega, computed in bohr^3 via volume()). Full lattice-vector node does not exist yet.

conventions: alat (celldm(1)) is always bohr
lattice vectors stored and re-emitted in units of alat
ibrav!=0 implies QE's own conventional orientation of primitive vectors (latgen)

entries.name: atomic-positions
entries.quantity: Atomic positions in the unit cell
entries.units: input card option: alat|bohr|angstrom|crystal|crystal_sg (default alat, deprecated); internally and in all phonon-chain files: cartesian in alat units

artifact.name: pw.x input (ATOMIC_POSITIONS card)
artifact.format_notes: dyn files and flfrc re-emit tau as cartesian coordinates in alat units regardless of the input option (write '(2i5,3f18.10)': index, type, tau).

source_anchors.what: ATOMIC_POSITIONS card with unit enum
tau written to dyn file (cartesian, alat units)
tau written to flfrc (same convention)

operation.notes: Together with the cell, fixes the crystal on which DFPT perturbations are applied.

maps_to.notes: No structure node in catalog.json; a CrystalStructure (or AtomicPositions) source-tier node would be needed to type this.

conventions: file formats always cartesian in alat units
input card may be crystal (fractional): conversion happens at read time

entries.name: atomic-species-and-masses
entries.quantity: Atomic species labels and masses
entries.units: amu (mass of C = 12) in the ATOMIC_SPECIES card

artifact.name: pw.x input (ATOMIC_SPECIES card)
artifact.format_notes: Masses are not used by the SCF itself but are stored in the data files; phonon calculations use these values unless overridden by amass in the ph.x/matdyn.x namelists. CRITICAL: when re-emitted in TEXT dyn files and text flfrc they are converted to Rydberg atomic units (amass*amu_ry, amu_ry = AMU_AU/2 = 911.444...); XML dyn/IFC files keep amu.

source_anchors.what: ATOMIC_SPECIES card: Mass_X in amu, note that phonon calculations reuse stored values
text dyn header writes amu_ry*amass(nt)
AMU_RY = AMU_AU / 2.0_DP

operation.notes: Enters only at dynamical-matrix diagonalization (mass weighting), never in the stored FC.

maps_to.notes: Direct match to the AtomicMass parameter node. Unit is amu at input; beware the file-format-dependent re-emission units.

conventions: amu at input
Ry atomic units (x 911.444 per amu) inside text dyn/flfrc files
amu inside XML dyn/IFC files

entries.name: pseudopotentials
entries.quantity: Pseudopotential per species (defines the electron-ion interaction and, with the XC functional, the Born-Oppenheimer potential)
entries.units: UPF file (Rydberg atomic units internally)

artifact.name: pseudo_dir/PseudoPot_X.UPF
artifact.format_notes: UPF format assumed. NC/US/PAW distinction changes which DFPT terms are active in ph.x (e.g. addusdynmat, drhodvus).

source_anchors.what: ATOMIC_SPECIES card: PseudoPot_X file, UPF format
pseudo_dir variable

operation.notes: Selects the specific realization of the BO potential together with the XC functional (input_dft).

maps_to.notes: Catalog's Potential is an opaque label for the BO potential; QE parameterizes it by pseudopotential + XC functional + cutoffs. Record those as the provenance of provide_potential.

conventions: UPF internal units are Rydberg atomic units

entries.name: planewave-cutoff
entries.quantity: Kinetic-energy cutoff for wavefunctions (and ecutrho for density)
entries.units: Ry

artifact.name: pw.x input (&SYSTEM ecutwfc, ecutrho)
artifact.format_notes: ecutrho defaults to 4*ecutwfc.

source_anchors.what: ecutwfc: kinetic energy cutoff (Ry) for wavefunctions, REQUIRED

operation.notes: Basis-set discretization; controls convergence of FC2, Z*, epsilon.

maps_to.notes: Discretization choice, not a Space; belongs in OperatorRepresentationSpec.discretization_choices for the provide_* edges.

conventions: Ry, not eV or Hartree

entries.name: kpoint-grid
entries.quantity: Electronic k-point sampling of the SCF (and of the DFPT k+q sums)
entries.units: grid divisions (dimensionless) or explicit points in 2pi/alat or crystal units per K_POINTS option

artifact.name: pw.x input (K_POINTS card)
artifact.format_notes: ph.x inherits the k set from the SCF; it must be compatible with each q (ph.x internally regenerates k, k+q).

source_anchors.what: K_POINTS card definition

operation.notes: Discretization of the BZ electron sums inside DFPT.

maps_to.notes: Discretization choice (bz_summation for the electronic problem), not a Space.

conventions: automatic grids are Monkhorst-Pack, optionally shifted

entries.name: scf-total-energy
entries.quantity: DFT total energy of the converged SCF ground state
entries.units: Ry on stdout; Hartree in data-file-schema.xml (etot/e2)

artifact.name: pw.x output ('!    total energy = ... Ry') and prefix.save/data-file-schema.xml
artifact.format_notes: The '!' marker prefixes the converged value. XML total_energy fields are divided by e2=2, i.e. Hartree atomic units, per the QES schema.

source_anchors.what: 9080 FORMAT total energy = F17.8 Ry
qexsd_init_total_energy(..., etot/e2, ...): XML in Hartree

operation.notes: Kohn-Sham self-consistency. Not consumed by the phonon chain directly, but its convergence level bounds FC quality.

maps_to.notes: No TotalEnergy node in catalog.json. Candidate new source-tier observable if EOS/QHA workflows are ever ingested.

conventions: stdout Ry vs XML Hartree (factor 2)
for smearing the printed quantity is the free energy F=E-TS variant flagged in output

entries.name: atomic-forces
entries.quantity: Forces on atoms (Hellmann-Feynman + corrections) from the SCF ground state
entries.units: Ry/au (Ry/bohr) on stdout; Hartree/bohr in data-file-schema.xml (force/e2)

artifact.name: 'Forces acting on atoms (cartesian axes, Ry/au):' block; also XML forces
artifact.format_notes: atom N type M force = 3F14.8. This is the quantity finite-displacement drivers (e.g. thirdorder.py for FC3, phonopy for FC2) harvest from pw.x supercell runs.

source_anchors.what: stdout header naming the unit Ry/au
9035 FORMAT per-atom force line
forces_aux = forces/e2: XML in Hartree/bohr

operation.notes: First derivative of E; the finite-difference route to force constants differentiates these numerically.

maps_to.notes: No Forces node. Needed if the finite-displacement FC2/FC3 provenance is to be modeled explicitly (thirdorder.py consumes exactly this).

conventions: 'Ry/au' means Ry/bohr
stdout Ry/bohr vs XML Hartree/bohr (factor 2)

entries.name: stress-tensor
entries.quantity: Macroscopic stress tensor of the SCF ground state
entries.units: Ry/bohr**3 and kbar, both printed side by side on stdout; Hartree/bohr^3 in XML (stress/e2)

artifact.name: 'total   stress  (Ry/bohr**3) ... (kbar)' block
artifact.format_notes: P = trace/3 printed in kbar via ry_kbar.

source_anchors.what: 9000 format: total stress (Ry/bohr**3) (kbar)
stress_aux = stress/e2: XML in Hartree atomic units

operation.notes: Relevant to the phonon chain as the relaxation-quality check (phonons assume zero-stress equilibrium) and to matdyn asr='all' Huang conditions (vanishing stress).

maps_to.notes: No Stress node in catalog.json.

conventions: dual units Ry/bohr^3 and kbar in the same block
sign: positive = compressive pressure printed as P

entries.name: scf-charge-density
entries.quantity: Converged ground-state charge density rho(G): the implicit input state of DFPT
entries.units: Rydberg atomic units (density e/bohr^3; stored as complex rho(G) Fourier coefficients, binary)
indices: G vector (spherical G-list up to ecutrho)

artifact.name: outdir/prefix.save/charge-density.dat
artifact.format_notes: Binary G-space record written by write_rhog. ph.x reads it back (read_file_ph) as the linear-response reference state; ph.x and pw.x must share outdir/prefix.

source_anchors.what: write_rhog(TRIM(dirname)//'charge-density', ...)
CALL read_file_ph: ph.x consumes the SCF state

operation.notes: Handoff artifact pw.x -> ph.x. Its pseudo-density character (US/PAW augmentation included) makes it code-internal.

maps_to.notes: Electronic-structure intermediate below the thermal DAG's current floor; if modeled, a hidden ChargeDensity node feeding provide_force_constants-like DFPT edges.

conventions: G-space, not real-space, storage
includes US/PAW augmentation contributions (not the bare valence density)

entries.name: kohn-sham-wavefunctions
entries.quantity: Converged Kohn-Sham orbitals at each k: second implicit input of DFPT (Sternheimer solver reference)
entries.units: Rydberg atomic units (binary plane-wave coefficients, normalized to 1 per band)

artifact.name: outdir/prefix.save/wfc*.dat
artifact.format_notes: One binary file per k point (write_wfc/write_collected_wfc). Arbitrary U(1)/degenerate-subspace phase per band: per-element values are not cross-code comparable.

source_anchors.what: SUBROUTINE write_collected_wfc
SUBROUTINE write_wfc (binary record layout)

operation.notes: Handoff artifact pw.x -> ph.x.

maps_to.notes: Hidden electronic intermediate; analogous gauge situation to the catalog's Eigenvectors node but electronic.

conventions: band phase gauge freedom
ph.x recomputes wavefunctions at k+q as needed

entries.name: ph-qgrid-parameters
entries.quantity: Phonon q-point grid request (ldisp mode)
entries.units: grid divisions (dimensionless)

artifact.name: ph.x input (&INPUTPH ldisp, nq1, nq2, nq3)
artifact.format_notes: ldisp=.true. runs DFPT on the irreducible wedge of a Monkhorst-Pack q-grid; single-q mode instead reads one xq line in 2pi/a0 units. lshift_q shifts the grid but is incompatible with q2r.x.

source_anchors.what: ldisp description
nq1, nq2, nq3 description
single-q input: xq in units of 2pi/a0

operation.notes: Determines the reciprocal-space FC2 sampling and hence the real-space FC2 supercell range after q2r (nr1,nr2,nr3 = nq1,nq2,nq3).

maps_to.notes: Discretization choice for the provide_force_constants[order=2] edge (analog of a supercell size in finite-displacement codes).

conventions: irreducible wedge only is computed; the star is reconstructed by symmetry
epsil is auto-set for insulators when ldisp=.true. (prepare_q.f90)

entries.name: ph-input-masses
entries.quantity: Atomic masses override for the dynamical matrix
entries.units: amu

artifact.name: ph.x input (&INPUTPH amass)
artifact.format_notes: Default 0.0 means: read masses from the pw.x data file (ATOMIC_SPECIES values).

source_anchors.what: amass(i): atomic mass [amu] of each atomic type; if not specified, read from data file

operation.notes: Only affects printed frequencies/patterns; the dyn-file FC block is mass-independent.

maps_to.notes: Same node as the pw.x masses; ph.x may override.

conventions: amu at input

entries.name: qgrid-header-file-dyn0
entries.quantity: The q-grid bookkeeping file: grid divisions and the list of irreducible q-points
entries.units: q in cartesian coordinates, units 2pi/alat

artifact.name: fildyn0 (e.g. alas.dyn0)
artifact.format_notes: Line 1: nq1 nq2 nq3 (3i4). Line 2: nqs (i4). Then nqs lines x_q (3e24.15). q2r.x requires this file (or manual nr1 nr2 nr3 + file list).

source_anchors.what: WRITE (iudyn,'(3i4)') nq1,nq2,nq3 then nqs then x_q list
fildyn0 contains information on the q-point grid (q2r side)

operation.notes: Pure bookkeeping between ph.x and q2r.x.

maps_to.notes: Provenance metadata for the FC2 edge, not a physical Space.

conventions: q in 2pi/alat cartesian, not crystal coordinates
example_instances: example02 produces alas.dyn0 with 8 q-points of the 4x4x4 grid

entries.name: dynamical-matrix-file
entries.quantity: DFPT force-constant matrix at q: C_ab^ij(q), the Bloch sum of real-space FC2. NOT mass-weighted, NOT NAC-corrected
entries.symbol: C(q) (QE calls it 'Dynamical Matrix in cartesian axes')
entries.units: Ry/bohr^2 (Rydberg atomic units; no unit string is printed in the file)
indices: q in star of q_irr (one block per star member)

artifact.name: fildyn{N} text (e.g. alas.dyn1..8) or fildyn{N}.xml
artifact.format_notes: Text: header (title; ntyp nat ibrav celldm; optional 'Basis vectors'; species with masses in Ry a.u. = amu*amu_ry; tau in alat) then one 'Dynamical  Matrix in cartesian axes' block per q in the star: q in 2pi/alat, then nat*nat blocks '(na nb)' of 3 rows x 3 (Re,Im) pairs phi(i,j,na,nb) '(3(2(f12.8,1x),2x))'. At q=0 with epsil: 'Dielectric Tensor:' (3f24.12) and 'Effective Charges E-U: Z_{alpha}{s,beta}' per atom. Tail: 'Diagonalizing the dynamical matrix', freq list, and normalized displacement patterns per mode. XML variant (always used for noncollinear/spin-orbit): GEOMETRY_INFO (MASS in amu!), DIELECTRIC_PROPERTIES (EPSILON, ZSTAR), DYNAMICAL_MAT_.{iq} (Q_POINT, PHI.na.nb), FREQUENCIES_THZ_CMM1 tail. Consumed by q2r.x (read_dyn_from_file / read_dyn_mat) and dynmat.x.

source_anchors.what: write_dyn_on_file: phi(3,3,nat,nat) complex written per q
header masses written as amu_ry*amass (Ry atomic units)
q2qstar_ph rotates and writes the dyn matrices of the whole star of q
XML: PHI.na.nb tags (same un-mass-weighted phi)
XML: MASS tag holds amass(nt) in amu (no amu_ry factor)

operation.notes: Second energy derivative from DFPT self-consistent linear response; symmetrized, star-expanded. Divide by sqrt(M_a M_b) (with M in Ry a.u. = amu*amu_ry) to obtain the catalog's mass-weighted dynamical matrix whose eigenvalues are omega^2 in Ry^2.

maps_to.notes: Matches BareDynamicalMatrix ('analytic Bloch sum of Phi(R) before NAC') up to the mass-weighting convention: QE stores the un-mass-weighted C(q). A declared normalization (mass_weighting=none vs sqrt_mass) is required; the numerical trap is a factor amu_ry*sqrt(m_a m_b) per element.

conventions: NOT mass-weighted (raw force constants at q)
NO non-analytic q->0 term included; Z*/epsilon are stored separately in the q=0 file
one file contains ALL q of the star of the irreducible q
text masses in Ry a.u., XML masses in amu
q vector in 2pi/alat cartesian
complex values as (Re, Im) pairs

entries.name: phonon-frequencies-at-q
entries.quantity: Harmonic phonon frequencies at the computed q (exact DFPT, no interpolation)
entries.units: THz and cm-1, printed side by side ('freq (i) = x [THz] = y [cm-1]'); both are LINEAR frequency nu = omega/2pi, not angular

artifact.name: ph.x stdout and tail of each fildyn file; XML tag FREQUENCIES_THZ_CMM1
artifact.format_notes: Negative printed value encodes omega^2 < 0 (imaginary/unstable mode): w1 = -sqrt(|w2|).

source_anchors.what: WRITE 9010: w1*RY_TO_THZ, w1*RY_TO_CMM1; lines 92-93 negative-sign convention
RY_TO_THZ = 1/AU_PS/FPI (the 1/4pi makes it linear frequency per Ry)
XML FREQUENCIES_THZ_CMM1 tail, same conversions

operation.notes: dyndia.f90 divides by masses (amu input) and diagonalizes; w2 eigenvalues are in Ry^2 (Rydberg atomic units with hbar=1).

maps_to.notes: Direct match. Declare linear-frequency convention: 1 QE THz = 2pi rad/ps; RY_TO_THZ = 3289.842 THz/Ry, RY_TO_CMM1 = 109737.57 cm-1/Ry.

conventions: linear (nu), not angular (omega) frequency
negative value = imaginary mode
these are exact-q DFPT values; matdyn.x values at the same q may differ slightly through Fourier interpolation and ASR
example_instances: example02 AlAs Gamma: TO 375.5 cm-1; LO 410.6 cm-1 appears only after NAC is applied downstream

entries.name: phonon-displacement-patterns-at-q
entries.quantity: Normalized displacement patterns per mode (eigenvector / sqrt(mass), renormalized)
entries.units: dimensionless (normalized complex vectors)

artifact.name: mode section at the tail of fildyn; XML DISPLACEMENT tags
artifact.format_notes: Per mode: '( 6f10.6 )' = 3 complex components per atom line. These are NOT orthonormal eigenvectors: z/sqrt(amu_ry*amass) renormalized to unit norm.

source_anchors.what: dyn(mu,nu) = z(mu,nu)/sqrt(amu_ry*amass); line 107-108 normalized write

operation.notes: U(1) phase per mode and U(d) freedom in degenerate subspaces; per-element values not cross-code comparable.

maps_to.notes: Maps to the hidden Eigenvectors node with an extra declared normalization: displacement-pattern convention (mass-divided, renormalized), distinct from orthonormal eigenvectors.

conventions: displacement patterns, not eigenvectors: not mutually orthogonal
renormalized to unit norm after mass division

entries.name: born-effective-charges-zeu
entries.quantity: Born effective charge tensor from the electric-field response (dF/dE route)
entries.units: units of elementary charge e (dimensionless numbers)

artifact.name: q=0 fildyn section 'Effective Charges E-U: Z_{alpha}{s,beta}'; stdout block 'Effective charges (d Force / dE)'; XML ZSTAR tag
artifact.format_notes: Computed when epsil=.true. and zeu=.true. (zeu defaults to epsil). Written WITHOUT acoustic sum rule; stdout additionally prints an ASR-corrected copy (mean subtracted). Row index is the E-field direction: rows printed as Ex/Ey/Ez.

source_anchors.what: dyn-file write: Z_{alpha}{s,beta} per atom, 3f24.12
stdout: 'Effective charges (d Force / dE) ... without acoustic sum rule applied'
zeu flag, default zeu=epsil

operation.closed_form: Z*_{s,alpha beta} = Omega/e * dP_alpha/du_{s beta} = dF_{s beta}/dE_alpha
operation.notes: QE's default algorithm computes it from the force response to the E-field perturbation.

maps_to.notes: Direct match to the source-tier BornCharges node (units of e). Index-order convention (field first) must be declared; equals the transpose of the dP/du layout.

conventions: raw, no ASR in the file (zasr applied later in q2r, or by hand)
index order: (E-field alpha, atom s, displacement beta)
insulators only
example_instances: example02 AlAs: Z*(Al) = +1.88, Z*(As) = -1.88 e (reference alas.ph.out)

entries.name: born-effective-charges-zue
entries.quantity: Born effective charge tensor from the displacement response (dP/du route), consistency alternative to zeu
entries.units: units of elementary charge e (dimensionless numbers)
indices: displacement direction (row of stdout Px/Py/Pz block)

artifact.name: 'Effective charges (d P / du) in cartesian axis' block
artifact.format_notes: Requires zue=.true. (default .false.) plus trans and epsil. NOT written to the dyn file; the dyn file always carries the zeu tensor. Storage is transposed relative to zstareu: zstarue(ipol, na, jpol).

source_anchors.what: stdout write of zstarue (d P / du)
zue flag: alternative algorithm; results should agree within numerical noise
accumulation of zstarue from phonon density responses

operation.closed_form: Z*_{s, beta alpha} = Omega/e * dP_alpha/du_{s beta}
operation.notes: Exact-DFPT equality zeu = zue^T holds analytically; the numerical difference is a convergence diagnostic.

maps_to.notes: Same node as zeu; a second producing route. Cross-checking the two is an internal EXPECTED_AGREE pair inside QE itself.

conventions: transposed storage vs zeu
stdout only, never in dyn/flfrc files

entries.name: dielectric-tensor-eps-inf
entries.quantity: Macroscopic high-frequency (electronic, clamped-ion) dielectric tensor
entries.units: dimensionless

artifact.name: q=0 fildyn section 'Dielectric Tensor:'; stdout 'Dielectric constant in cartesian axis'; XML EPSILON tag
artifact.format_notes: Computed when epsil=.true. at q=0 for insulators (auto-enabled for insulators when ldisp=.true., see prepare_q.f90). Variants lrpa (DV_xc=0) and lnoloc (DV_Hxc=0) change the flavor and are labeled in stdout.

source_anchors.what: dyn-file 'Dielectric Tensor:' 3f24.12
stdout header (plus RPA/noloc variants at lines 33-35)
epsil flag description

operation.notes: Clamped-ion electronic response; the ionic (static) contribution is a separate downstream construct (dynmat.x), out of this slice.

maps_to.notes: Direct match to the source-tier DielectricTensor (eps_inf) node consumed by apply_nac_correction.

conventions: high-frequency (electronic) epsilon, not static eps_0
insulators only; code stops if metallic occupations
example_instances: example02 AlAs: 13.742 (LDA overestimates the experimental 8.16; documented in QE examples)

entries.name: dvscf-response-potential
entries.quantity: Self-consistent potential response to each phonon perturbation (per irrep pattern)
entries.units: Rydberg atomic units (binary; potential variation per unit displacement of the irrep pattern)

artifact.name: outdir/_ph0/prefix.fildvscf1 (and fildrho for the density response)
artifact.format_notes: Written when fildvscf /= '' ; primary consumer is electron-phonon post-processing (EPW, elphon). Basis is the irreducible-representation pattern basis, not cartesian displacements.

source_anchors.what: fildvscf: file for the deltavscf to be used in electron-phonon calculations
fildrho: saved as outdir/_ph0/prefix.fildrho1

operation.notes: Intermediate of the Sternheimer SCF loop, persisted for reuse.

maps_to.notes: Electron-phonon scaffolding beyond the current thermal DAG; hidden node candidate only if e-ph transport is ever ingested.

conventions: pattern (irrep) basis, requires the patterns file to rotate to cartesian
exact per-record normalization not fully traced in this scan (see open questions)

entries.name: real-space-ifc-file
entries.quantity: Real-space interatomic force constants C(R) on the nr1 x nr2 x nr3 supercell grid (short-range part only for polar solids)
entries.units: Ry/bohr^2 (Rydberg atomic units; no unit string in the file)

artifact.name: flfrc (e.g. alas444.fc) or flfrc.xml
artifact.format_notes: Text layout: ntyp nat ibrav celldm; optional at if ibrav=0; species lines (mass in Ry a.u., copied verbatim from the text dyn file); tau cartesian alat; line 'lrigid alph' (T/F + Ewald alpha); if lrigid: epsil 3x3 then per-atom zeu 3x3; nr1 nr2 nr3; then 3*3*nat*nat blocks headed 'i j na nb' with nr1*nr2*nr3 lines 'm1 m2 m3 value' (real part only; imaginary checked < 1e-12). With write_lr=.true. a second column holds the long-range IFC (needed by matdyn asr='all' + read_lr). XML variant: INTERATOMIC_FORCE_CONSTANTS with MESH_NQ1_NQ2_NQ3, alpha_ewald, IFC and optional IFC_LR tags, masses in amu. Consumed by matdyn.x (readfc / read_ifc).

source_anchors.what: rgd_blk(..., -1.d0): long-range dipole term SUBTRACTED from C(q) before the FFT when Z* present
text flfrc write block (header through IFC loops at 358-382)
masses written as read from text dyn file, i.e. still in Ry atomic units
matdyn readfc: amass = amass_from_file/amu_ry (text path only)
XML write_ifc: IFC / IFC_LR tags

operation.notes: Inverse FFT of the q-grid force constants after subtracting the Gonze rigid-ion (dipole-dipole) long-range term for polar solids (X. Gonze et al., PRB 50, 13035 (1994) formula in rigid.f90:rgd_blk). matdyn re-adds the term analytically at arbitrary q.

maps_to.notes: This is QE's grounding of the FC2 leaf the thermal DAG takes as given. TWO declared conventions required: (1) unit Ry/bohr^2 (1 Ry/bohr^2 = 48.58681221205054 eV/A^2 vs eV/A^2 codes); (2) for polar materials the array is SHORT-RANGE only; total FC2 = frc + dipole term(Z*, eps, alph). Naive comparison with e.g. phonopy FORCE_CONSTANTS is wrong whenever Z* != 0.

conventions: Ry/bohr^2
short-range only when Z* present (lrigid=T)
index nesting i,j,na,nb outermost, m1 fastest innermost
text masses Ry a.u. / XML masses amu
real part only stored
alph Ewald parameter (default 1.0) written next to lrigid; older files lack it and readfc falls back

entries.name: zasr-born-charge-sum-rule
entries.quantity: Acoustic sum rule enforcement on the Born effective charges before writing flfrc
entries.units: enum: no|simple|crystal|one-dim|zero-dim

artifact.name: q2r.x input (&INPUT zasr)
artifact.format_notes: Default 'no': the raw DFPT Z* (which violate sum Z*=0 by numerical noise) pass through unchanged.

source_anchors.what: zasr enum and semantics
CALL set_zasr(zasr, ...) applied to zeu when lrigid

operation.closed_form: sum_s Z*_s = 0 enforced by the selected projection
operation.notes: Charge-neutrality counterpart of the FC acoustic sum rule.

maps_to.notes: A normalization scheme on the BornCharges representation, not a Space; belongs in scheme_overrides of the provide_born_charges edge.

conventions: applied to Z* only; FC ASR is a separate matdyn-side choice (asr)

entries.name: matdyn-asr-mode
entries.quantity: Acoustic sum rule enforcement on the real-space FC2 before interpolation
entries.units: enum: no|simple|crystal|all|one-dim|zero-dim (+ huang logical)

artifact.name: matdyn.x input (&INPUT asr, huang)
artifact.format_notes: Default 'no'. 'crystal' = translational ASR by projection; 'all' adds rotational + 15 Huang invariance conditions (requires write_lr in q2r and read_lr in matdyn for infrared-active solids; cite npj Comput Mater 8, 236 (2022)).

source_anchors.what: asr enum and semantics
CALL set_asr(asr, nr1,nr2,nr3, frc, frc_lr, zeu, ...)
warning: asr='all' with Z* requires read_lr/write_lr

operation.closed_form: sum_b C_ab(R) corrections such that acoustic omega(q->0) -> 0
operation.notes: Applied in real space each matdyn run; the flfrc file itself remains raw unless write_frc=.true. rewrites it as flfrc.matdyn.

maps_to.notes: Scheme on the compute_dynamical_matrix edge (ASR normalization of FC2), not a Space.

conventions: default is NO sum rule: raw DFPT FC give small nonzero acoustic frequencies at Gamma

entries.name: matdyn-input-masses
entries.quantity: Atomic mass override for interpolation (mass-approximation runs)
entries.units: amu
indices: species index (of the possibly enlarged supercell type list)

artifact.name: matdyn.x input (&INPUT amass)
artifact.format_notes: Default: use masses read from flfrc. Text flfrc masses are divided by amu_ry on read (they are stored in Ry a.u.); XML flfrc masses are used as-is (amu).

source_anchors.what: amass: masses of atoms in the supercell (a.m.u.)
text path: amass(nt) = amass_from_file/amu_ry
XML path: amass_blk used directly (amu), no division

operation.notes: Enters at dyndiag only; enables isotope mass-approximation dispersions from a single FC file.

maps_to.notes: Same AtomicMass node, third override point in the chain.

conventions: amu at input; file storage differs by format (Ry a.u. text, amu XML)

entries.name: matdyn-qpoint-path
entries.quantity: List of q-vectors (band path or explicit list) at which to interpolate
entries.units: cartesian coordinates in 2pi/a units (a = lattice parameter); crystal coordinates if q_in_cryst_coord=.true.

artifact.name: matdyn.x input q card (nq then q lines; band form with nptq counts if q_in_band_form)
artifact.format_notes: For q=0 entries the NAC direction qhat is inferred from adjacent list points: qhat = q(n)-q(n-1) or q(n)-q(n+1); to probe two limits list q=0 twice. dos=.true. replaces the list with an internal nk1 x nk2 x nk3 grid.

source_anchors.what: q-points in cartesian 2pi/a units
qhat direction rule for the q=0 non-analytic limit
implementation of the qhat = q(n)-q(n±1) rule

operation.notes: Defines the interpolation targets; also implicitly parameterizes the direction-dependent LO-TO limit at Gamma.

maps_to.notes: Discretization/evaluation-set choice, not a Space.

conventions: 2pi/alat cartesian by default
crystal input converted to cartesian on read
q=0 duplicated to sample multiple LO-TO limits

entries.name: interpolated-phonon-frequencies
entries.quantity: Fourier-interpolated phonon dispersion frequencies at the requested q list
entries.units: cm-1 (negative value = imaginary mode, omega^2 < 0)

artifact.name: flfrq (default matdyn.freq) + flfrq.gp + flfrq.rap
artifact.format_notes: flfrq: header '&plot nbnd= x, nks= y /' then per q: 'q_x q_y q_z wq' (2pi/alat cartesian + weight) and 3*nat frequencies (6f10.4) in cm-1. flfrq.gp: gnuplot-friendly, column 1 = cumulative |dq| path length in 2pi/alat units, then 3*nat cm-1 columns. flfrq.rap (XML IFC only): irrep labels per mode.

source_anchors.what: freq = SQRT(ABS(w2))*RY_TO_CMM1 with negative-sign rule at 770
flfrq '&plot' header and per-q writes
flfrq.gp path-length variant

operation.closed_form: D(q) = (FFT of frc with WS weights) [+ rgd_blk LR term] [+ nonanal at q=0]; omega = sqrt(eig)
operation.notes: frc_blk uses Wigner-Seitz weighting (wsweight) over a 2x grid; rgd_blk re-adds the dipole term with sign +1 when has_zstar (matdyn.f90:1280).

maps_to.notes: Same Frequency node as ph.x exact-q values, different producing operation (interpolation vs direct DFPT); cm-1 only in this file (no THz column).

conventions: cm-1, linear wavenumber
negative = imaginary
LO-TO handling at q=0 depends on qhat from the path (see matdyn-qpoint-path) and on loto_disable
example_instances: example02 reference alas.freq: '&plot nbnd= 6, nks= 161 /', Gamma row '-0.0000 -0.0000 0.0000 375.5172 375.5172 410.5598' (LO-TO split visible)

entries.name: phonon-dos
entries.quantity: Phonon density of states (plus per-atom projected DOS columns)
entries.units: DOS in states/cm-1 vs omega in cm-1; normalized so integral = 3*nat
indices: columns: E, total DOS, PDOS per atom

artifact.name: fldos (default matdyn.dos)
artifact.format_notes: Header '# Frequency[cm^-1] DOS PDOS'; rows '(2ES18.10,1000ES12.4)': E, sum DOS, then nat atom-projected columns. Grid nk1 x nk2 x nk3 (must be given when dos=.true.), tetrahedron integration by default (degauss=0), Gaussian broadening of width degauss (cm-1) otherwise; energy step deltaE (cm-1, default 1) or ndos.

source_anchors.what: header write '# Frequency[cm^-1] DOS PDOS'
row write: E, SUM(DOSofE), DOSofE(1:nat)
fldos doc: dos in states/cm-1 vs cm-1, normalised to 3*nat

operation.closed_form: g(E) = sum_nu int_BZ delta(E - omega_nu(q)) dq (tetrahedra dos_gam, or Gaussian dos_broad)
operation.notes: PDOS weight is |e_na|^2 with orthonormal eigenvectors reconstructed via zq = z*sqrt(amu_ry*amass) (matdyn.f90:698).

maps_to.notes: Direct match. Catalog normalization (1/N_q sum -> integral 3*nat) agrees with QE's documented normalization; axis unit is cm-1.

conventions: states/cm-1 (per unit cell), integral 3*nat
tetrahedra by default, Gaussian if degauss > 0 (degauss in cm-1)
PDOS columns appended after the total

entries.name: normalized-phonon-displacements
entries.quantity: Per-mode normalized displacement patterns at each interpolated q
entries.units: dimensionless normalized complex vectors; per-mode headers repeat frequencies in THz and cm-1

artifact.name: flvec (default matdyn.modes)
artifact.format_notes: writemodes: eigenvectors divided by sqrt(mass) then renormalized: NOT orthogonal (explicitly documented). Same '(3(f10.6,1x,f10.6,3x))' complex triple format as dyn-file tails.

source_anchors.what: writemodes: z/znorm written (z from dyndiag is already mass-divided)
flvec doc: eigenvectors / sqrt(mass), normalized, not orthogonal

operation.notes: Gauge freedom identical to Eigenvectors.

maps_to.notes: Hidden node; displacement-pattern normalization must be declared (differs from fleig by sqrt(amu_ry*m) per component).

conventions: mass-divided and renormalized (displacement patterns)
default-on artifact (flvec='matdyn.modes')

entries.name: phonon-eigenvectors
entries.quantity: Per-mode orthonormal eigenvectors of the mass-weighted dynamical matrix at each interpolated q
entries.units: dimensionless orthonormal complex vectors; per-mode headers in THz and cm-1

artifact.name: fleig (default ' ', i.e. off; conventionally matdyn.eig)
artifact.format_notes: write_eigenvectors multiplies dyndiag's displacement output back by sqrt(amu_ry*amass): these ARE orthogonal (documented).

source_anchors.what: z_ = z*sqrt(amu_ry*amass): reconstruct orthonormal eigenvectors
fleig doc: eigenvectors of the dynamical matrix, orthogonal

operation.notes: The flvec/fleig pair is QE's explicit realization of the displacement-vs-eigenvector convention split.

maps_to.notes: Hidden node, orthonormal-eigenvector normalization (the catalog's canonical choice for e).

conventions: orthonormal
off by default

entries.name: interpolated-dynamical-matrix
entries.quantity: Interpolated force-constant matrix C(q) at each requested q, including the non-analytic term at q=0 when applicable
entries.units: Ry/bohr^2 (same write_dyn_on_file format as ph.x dyn files; not mass-weighted)

artifact.name: fldyn (default ' ', off)
artifact.format_notes: One 'Dynamical  Matrix in cartesian axes' block per q; at q=0 the epsilon/Z* section is appended too. Unlike ph.x dyn files this matrix ALREADY contains the rgd_blk long-range re-addition and the nonanal q->0 term for the path direction.

source_anchors.what: call write_dyn_on_file(q, dyn, nat, iout_dyn) after setupmat and nonanal
write_epsilon_and_zeu appended at q=0

operation.notes: The NAC-inclusive counterpart of the ph.x dyn file.

maps_to.notes: Corresponds to the post-NAC DynamicalMatrix node, again modulo the mass-weighting convention (QE stores un-mass-weighted C; the node's frequency^2 form requires the amu_ry*sqrt(m m) division).

conventions: not mass-weighted
includes NAC (difference from ph.x fildyn)
off by default

entries.name: nac-loto-splitting
entries.quantity: Non-analytic q->0 correction (LO-TO splitting) applied during interpolation. NOTE-ONLY entry per scan scope
entries.units: Ry/bohr^2 (added to C(q) before mass division; e^2 = 2 in Rydberg units)

artifact.name: behavioral: modifies fldyn/flfrq/flvec outputs at q=0
artifact.format_notes: nonanal (rigid.f90) adds the Gonze rigid-ion G=0 term along qhat; rgd_blk handles the full G-sum long-range part at general q (added with sign +1 in setupmat, subtracted with sign -1 in q2r). Controls: loto_disable (skip at q=0), loto_2d (2D treatment, fac = 2pi e^2/Area with effective screening reff), na_ifc (finite-displacement variant, Wang PRB 85, 224303 (2012)). If Z* absent from flfrc: warning 'TO-LO splitting at q=0 will be absent'.

source_anchors.what: nonanal: dyn += fpi*e2*zag(i)*zbg(j)/qeq/omega
IF (lo_to_split) CALL nonanal(...)
setupmat: rgd_blk(..., +1.d0) re-adds the long-range term
reference: X. Gonze et al, PRB 50, 13035 (1994)

operation.notes: QE's realization of the catalog's apply_nac_correction edge: BareDynamicalMatrix + BornCharges + DielectricTensor -> DynamicalMatrix. Direction qhat comes from the q-list sequence.

maps_to.notes: Grounds the apply_nac_correction edge exactly (Gonze convention; e^2=2 Ry units internally, cancels in the dimensionless result).

conventions: Gonze 1994 dipole-dipole form (same family as phonopy's default NAC)
direction-dependent at Gamma: value depends on path geometry
split into smooth G-sum part (rgd_blk, all q) + nonanal Gamma limit
example_instances: example02 AlAs Gamma: TO 375.5 / LO 410.6 cm-1

entries.name: fc3-third-order-route
entries.quantity: Third-order (anharmonic) force constants. NOTE-ONLY entry: NOT produced by this QE phonon chain
entries.units: n/a in this slice (external: thirdorder.py emits eV/A^3 ShengBTE format; D3Q emits its own anh* files in Ry atomic units)

artifact.name: external: FORCE_CONSTANTS_3RD (thirdorder.py) or mat3R/anh files (D3Q)
artifact.format_notes: Two external routes: (1) finite differences: thirdorder.py generates displaced supercells, pw.x supplies atomic-forces (Ry/au) for each, thirdorder.py assembles FC3; (2) DFPT: the D3Q code (superseding the old QE D3 code, per PHonon user guide) computes the third derivative directly from ph.x-adjacent linear response. Neither artifact is written by pw.x/ph.x/q2r.x/matdyn.x in this repo tree.

source_anchors.what: old D3 code 'superseded by the D3Q code, available on https://sourceforge.net/projects/d3q/'
the pw.x forces that the finite-difference FC3 route consumes

operation.notes: In the openmaterials map QE's role for FC3 is indirect: provider of forces (finite-difference route) or host of the D3Q extension (not vendored here).

maps_to.notes: The FC3 node stays grounded by phono3py/ShengBTE adapters; QE grounds only its inputs. Beware the known phono3py-vs-ShengBTE eV/A^3 0.1 factor documented in docs/openmaterials.pdf Appendix A before chaining QE-forces -> thirdorder.py -> ShengBTE.

conventions: not produced in-tree
unit conventions belong to the external tool, not QE

open_questions: fildvscf record normalization: the potential response is stored in the irreducible-representation pattern basis; the exact per-unit-displacement scaling (bohr vs pattern norm) was not traced to the write site in this scan. EPW's reader (not in scope) is the authoritative decoder.

flfrq wq column: matdyn.f90:778 always writes a 4th value wq(n) after the q vector; in band-path mode (dos=.false.) wq is allocated but its initialization path was not fully traced (older reference outputs, e.g. example02 alas.freq, show only 3 values, so the column was added later). Harmless for parsing frequencies but the weight semantics off the DOS grid are unverified.

PhononDOS normalization was verified from INPUT_MATDYN.txt (states/cm-1, integral = 3*nat) and the tetrahedron weight structure, but not validated numerically against a reference integral in this scan.
Which QE ASR flavor ('simple' vs 'crystal') corresponds to phonopy's default FC symmetrization needs a numerical cross-check before declaring EXPECTED_AGREE on near-Gamma acoustic frequencies.

The XML dyn format is auto-selected for noncollinear/spin-orbit runs (fildyn gains .xml); whether ldisp text runs can also be forced to XML (control_ph xmldyn) and whether both are ever written simultaneously was not chased.
matdyn can also run q2r internally when fildyn is given in its namelist (matdyn.f90:347-354, do_q2r call); the catalog treats q2r.x as the canonical producer and this shortcut as an alias.
Stress-tensor sign convention (tension-positive vs compression-positive) for the printed sigma was not verified against a strained reference; only the unit duality (Ry/bohr^3, kbar) was anchored.
